sections.title: Record-keeping requirements for client relationships and transactions
sections.summary: Accountable institutions must maintain detailed records of client identities, relationships, transactions, and related documentation. This includes verifying the identity of persons acting on behalf of clients, the nature of transactions, amounts involved, and all accounts linked to transactions. sections.source_quote: 26. (1) An authorised representative of the Centre has access during ordinary working hours to any records kept by or on behalf of an accountable institution in terms of section 22 or section 24, and may examine, make extracts from or copies of, any such records. (2) The authorised representative of the Centre may, except in the case of records which the public is entitled to have access to, exercise the powers mentioned in subsection (1) only by virtue of a warrant issued in chambers by a magistrate or regional magistrate or judge of an area of jurisdiction within which the records or any of them are kept, or within which the accountable institution conducts business. (3) A warrant may only be issued if it appears to the judge, magistrate or regional magistrate from information on oath or affirmation that there are reasonable grounds to believe that the records referred to in subsection (1) may assist the Centre to identify the proceeds of unlawful activities or to combat money laundering activities.

sections.heading: An  accountable institution and a reporting institution must,  within the prescribed
sections.title: Reporting of cash transactions exceeding prescribed amounts
sections.summary: An accountable institution or reporting institution must report to the Financial Intelligence Centre (FIC) within the prescribed period any transaction involving cash payments or receipts exceeding the prescribed amount to or from a client. The reporting applies when the institution pays or receives cash exceeding the threshold from or to a client, or a person acting on behalf of the client. The section specifies the conditions under which reporting obligations arise for cash transactions.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires reporting of cross-border cash transactions exceeding thresholds to the FIC, impacting data transfer assessments for financial institutions handling international cash movements.

requirements.text: An accountable institution or reporting institution must report to the Centre within the prescribed period the prescribed particulars concerning a transaction involving cash paid or received exceeding the prescribed amount to or from a client.

sections.source_quote: 28. An accountable institution and a reporting institution must, within the prescribed period, report to the Centre the prescribed particulars concerning a transaction concluded with a client if in terms of the transaction an amount of cash in excess of the prescribed amount is paid by the accountable institution or reporting institution to the client, or to a person acting on behalf of the client, or to a person on whose behalf the client is acting; or is received by the accountable institution or reporting institution from the client, or from a person acting on behalf of the client, or from a person on whose behalf the client is acting.

sections.heading: (1) A person who carries on a businl:ss or is in charge of or  manages a business  or
sections.title: Reporting suspicious transactions involving unlawful activities
sections.summary: A person who carries on a business or manages a business must report to the FIC within the prescribed period if they know or suspect that a transaction involves proceeds of unlawful activities, lacks apparent lawful purpose, avoids reporting duties, or relates to tax evasion. The reporting obligation applies when the person has knowledge or suspicion of such transactions, and includes providing grounds for the knowledge or suspicion and transaction details. The section also prohibits disclosure of reports to third parties except under specific legal conditions.
sections.dtia_summary: Mandates reporting of suspicious transactions to the FIC, affecting data transfer assessments for financial institutions handling cross-border transactions with potential money laundering risks.

requirements.text: A person who carries on a business or manages a business must report to the Centre within the prescribed period the grounds for knowledge or suspicion and prescribed particulars concerning a transaction if they know or suspect it involves proceeds of unlawful activities, lacks apparent lawful purpose, avoids reporting duties, or relates to tax evasion.

A person who knows or suspects a transaction may have caused consequences of section 29(1) must report to the Centre within the prescribed period the grounds for knowledge or suspicion and transaction details.

No person who makes a report in terms of this section may disclose the fact or report contents to any other person except within the scope of their powers, for carrying out this Act, legal proceedings, or court orders.

sections.heading: For the purposes of sections 65 and 66-
sections.title: Definitions for access, application, computer system, and data
sections.summary: This section provides precise definitions for key terms used in sections 65 and 66, including 'access', 'application', 'computer system', and 'data'. These definitions clarify the scope of unauthorized access and system modification provisions.
sections.source_quote: 67. For the purposes of sections 65 and 66- "access" in relation to an application or data means rendering that application or data, by whatever means, in a form that would enable a person, at the time when it is so rendered or subsequently, to take account of that application or data, and includes using the application or data or having it output from the computer system in which it is held in a displayed or printed form or to a storage medium or by means of any output device, whether attached to the computer system in which the application or data are held or not; "application" means a set of instructions that, when executed in a computer system, causes a computer system to perform a function; "computer system" means an electronic, magnetic, optical, electrochemical or other data processing device, including the physical components thereof, and any removable storage medium that is for the time being therein or connected thereto, or a group of such interconnected or related devices, one or more of which is capable of- (i) containing data; or (ii) performing a logical, arithmetical or any other function in relation to data; "data" means any representation of information, knowledge, facts or concepts, capable of being processed in a computer system.
sections.relevance_reason: Definitions for data terms in FICA

sections.title: Search and seizure of cash for financial offences
